summary: Stopped at phase 1 pre-handoff verification. GKE access is available, but the expected Auth0 Secret Manager entries and Google Maps widget-key referrer are not in the state required by the bridge task.
blockers: Create or map the expected secrets `muno-auth0-domain`, `muno-auth0-client-id`, and `muno-auth0-audience` in project `noetl-demo-19700101`.
Grant the worker service account `noetl-worker-mcp@noetl-demo-19700101.iam.gserviceaccount.com` `roles/secretmanager.secretAccessor` on those secrets.
Add `https://muno.mestumre.dev/*` to the `travel-agent-widget-key` allowed HTTP referrers without removing the existing referrers.

Clarify deployment TLS topology: the cluster has Gateway API and ManagedCertificate resources, but no cert-manager `Certificate` CRD. The Round 8 deploy should mirror the existing gateway/Cloudflare pattern rather than assume cert-manager Ingress objects.
manual_fix_commands_for_kadyapam: for s in muno-auth0-domain muno-auth0-client-id muno-auth0-audience; do gcloud secrets add-iam-policy-binding "$s" --project=noetl-demo-19700101 --member=serviceAccount:noetl-worker-mcp@noetl-demo-19700101.iam.gserviceaccount.com --role=roles/secretmanager.secretAccessor; done
In Google Cloud Console, edit the API key `travel-agent-widget-key` and add the HTTP referrer `https://muno.mestumre.dev/*`.
verdict: AMBER. The round intentionally stopped before code or deploy changes because required pre-handoff checks failed. Once the three Auth0 secrets and Maps referrer are present, rerun Round 8 from phase 1.
